name: Mirae Asset Nifty India Consumption ETF

index.name: Nifty India Consumption Index
index.description: Nifty India Consumption tracks 30 companies benefiting from rising middle class and aspirational spending — structural multi-decade tailwind underpinned by 900M population below age 40 with ₹10L+ annual household income (650M low-income + 250M middle-income + 50M affluent). Includes FMCG (Unilever 13.5%), auto (Maruti 9.7%), retail (Avenue Supermarts DMART 6.5%, Trent 5.8%), jewellery (Titan 8.9%), paints (Asian Paints 7.3%), quick-service restaurants (Jubilant Domino's 4.2%). Dual domestic+export model: while domestic consumption captures rural-to-urban migration and income growth, auto/retail companies export (Maruti exports 40%, Trent international operations). Currency benefit: when rupee weakens, export revenues rise; when rupee strengthens, consumption companies benefit from cheaper imported raw materials. Secular trend: rural consumption converging toward urban patterns — while rural FMCG growth 12% vs urban 8%, rural penetration remains 40% vs global 65-70%, suggesting 20-year runway. Leverage cycle: when credit expansion accelerates (auto loans, retail financing), consumption stocks re-rate 20-30% as leverage multiplies turnover growth. Negative drivers: wealth effect recession (when stock market crashes, HNI spending recedes 30-40%).
